name: Umm Abdullah bint Hasan
title: Mother of Imam Baqir
shortDescription: Daughter of Imam Hasan (AS), wife of Imam Sajjad (AS), and mother of Imam Baqir (AS) who was present in Karbala and taken captive.
fullStory:
Umm Abdullah (Fatima) bint Hasan ibn Ali was a highly revered lady. She is the daughter of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS), wife of Imam Sajjad (AS), and mother of Imam Muhammad Baqir (AS).

She is the only woman whose husband was an Imam, father was an Imam, and son was an Imam. Umm Abdullah was present in Karbala with her sick husband (Imam Sajjad) and her young son (Imam Baqir, who was about 4 years old).

On Ashura, she witnessed the martyrdom of her uncle Imam Hussain (AS), her brothers (Qasim and Abdullah), and other loved ones. After Ashura, she was one of the brave women of the captives' caravan who traveled from Kufa to Sham in chains, nursing her sick husband and young son.

Imam Sadiq (AS) said about his grandmother: 'She was a truthful woman (Siddiqa), and no woman in the family of Imam Hasan (AS) reached her rank and status.'
quoteArabic: كانَتْ صِدِّيقَةً لَمْ تُدْرَكْ فِي آلِ الحَسَنِ امْرَأَةٌ مِثْلُها...
quote: (Imam Sadiq's saying about her:) She was a Siddiqa (truthful), no woman like her was found in Hasan's family.
